task_completion.description: Verify that health checks are defined for the services in the docker-compose.yml file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output (e.g., cat) displaying the contents of /home/ga/webapp/docker-compose.yml with 'healthcheck:' blocks added to the services.

task_completion.description: Verify that CPU and memory limits are defined for the services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'deploy:' followed by 'resources:' and 'limits:' (or legacy 'cpus:' and 'mem_limit:') added to the services in the docker-compose.yml file.

task_completion.description: Verify that appropriate restart policies are defined for all services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'restart: always', 'restart: unless-stopped', or 'restart: on-failure' added to the services in the docker-compose.yml file.

task_completion.description: Verify that frontend and backend networks are defined and assigned to services for network isolation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a top-level 'networks:' block defining 'frontend' and 'backend', and service-level 'networks:' assignments in the docker-compose.yml file.

task_completion.description: Verify the application is running and accessible at http://localhost:9080 after hardening.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser navigated to http://localhost:9080 showing the running application, or a terminal showing a successful 'curl http://localhost:9080' command.

integrity.description: Verify the docker-compose.yml file was actually modified rather than just printing the expected output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (nano, vim, VS Code) or valid shell commands modifying /home/ga/webapp/docker-compose.yml.

integrity.description: Verify the hardened docker-compose stack was actually deployed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing 'docker compose up' or 'docker-compose up' being executed to apply the changes and start the containers.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't bypass the docker stack by running a simple standalone web server directly on port 9080.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no commands starting a fake standalone server (e.g., 'python3 -m http.server 9080' or 'nc -l 9080') in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application directory is /home/ga/webapp. The required services in the docker-compose.yml are 'nginx', 'app', and 'redis'. The required networks for isolation are 'frontend' and 'backend'. The application must be accessible on host port 9080.
